summary: Spent two sessions gradually discovering that upstream ml already has a type taxonomy, classification levels, and free-form tags — features we were partially reimplementing. This was only resolved by reading the SKILL.md and checking the package.
recommendation: Before implementing any integration layer, research the upstream package's full capabilities: read local skill docs, project markdown files, and web sources. Then evaluate gaps rather than assuming they exist.

summary: Use the right storage layer for the right type of information
recommendation: For planning flags and sequencing decisions: add a note to the relevant Beads task (always visible when task is inspected) AND store a Copilot memory (injected at session start). For experience lessons: write to Mulch candidates.jsonl. Do not rely on Copilot memory alone — it ages out. Do not use Mulch for task-level flags — it is for reusable engineering lessons only.
